tasks.name: Empirical scan: Arabic back-reference patterns in fixtures (Defense 1A)
tasks.prompt:
Run the empirical back-reference scanner:
  python scripts/empirical_backrefs.py --output overnight/results/empirical-backrefs/scan.json

Read the JSON output and write an interpretation to overnight/results/empirical-backrefs/summary.md:
- Which patterns had the most hits?
- What is the hit rate per million characters?
- Is the rate sufficient to justify building Defense 1A (dangling reference detector)?
- What is the recommended decision (PROCEED or SKIP)?

tasks.name: SPEC rule → test coverage matrix for excerpting engine
tasks.prompt: Read engines/excerpting/SPEC.md section 4 and extract every behavioral rule (numbered items, MUST/SHALL statements, error codes). Then read all test files in engines/excerpting/tests/ and match each SPEC rule to its test(s). Write a coverage matrix to overnight/results/test-coverage-excerpting/coverage.md showing: (1) which rules have tests, (2) which rules lack tests, (3) rules with only partial coverage. Focus on §4 (Phase 1), §5 (Phase 2), §7 (Phase 3), and §8 (error codes).

tasks.name: Code quality scan: Arabic-unsafe patterns in excerpting engine
tasks.prompt:
Scan all Python files in engines/excerpting/src/ for:
1. Regex using \d instead of [0-9] (Arabic-Indic digit safety — see .claude/rules/regex-arabic-digits.md)
2. .lower()/.upper()/.strip() called on variables that might contain Arabic text
3. Bare except: handlers (must catch specific exceptions per CLAUDE.md)
4. Missing type hints on function signatures
5. Pydantic Field(None) pattern compliance (see .claude/rules/python-code.md)
6. Functions exceeding 50 lines

Also scan shared/*/src/ for the same patterns.
Write findings to overnight/results/cq-scan/scan.md with file:line for each issue.

tasks.name: Model role assignment research: compare Opus 4.6 / GPT-5.4 / Gemini 3.1 Pro on Arabic scholarly text
tasks.prompt:
Execute the model role assignment research defined in NEXT.md Task 1.

You are the model-researcher agent. Follow your full research protocol:
1. Load context: read NEXT.md Task 1, engines/excerpting/contracts.py lines 749-761, SPEC.md §7.3, docs/llm_trustworthiness_defenses.md
2. Run 8-10+ web searches for Arabic capability benchmarks per model
3. Evaluate each of the 3 candidates (anthropic/claude-opus-4.6, openai/gpt-5.4, google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview) for the 3 consensus roles:
   - Primary (structured scholarly extraction)
   - Verifier (error detection in someone else's output)
   - Escalation (adjudicate disagreements)
4. Design an empirical probe with 3 specific chunks from experiments/format_diversity_test/
5. Write structured findings to overnight/results/model-research/findings.md with:
   - Model capability matrix (Arabic scores, JSON reliability, pricing)
   - Recommended role assignments with evidence
   - Exact model strings for contracts.py
   - Cost analysis for 5-book run
   - Empirical probe design
   - Full evidence log (every search query and URL)

The different-provider requirement (SPEC §7.3) means Primary and Verifier MUST be from different providers.
All models must be available on OpenRouter.
Cite specific URLs and benchmark data for every claim.

tasks.name: Probe: layer merge at chunk split boundaries
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — Layer Merge at Chunk Split Boundaries

Read engines/excerpting/src/phase3_deterministic.py, focusing on _compute_layer_coverages() lines 120-140.

The CORRUPTION VECTOR: When text is split into chunks (split_info is not None), layer_split_points contains ABSOLUTE offsets from the original assembled_text. But the chunk's text_layers have RELATIVE offsets (from the chunk's start). The merge condition checks `merged[-1][2] in split_set` — but split_set contains absolute offsets while layer.start/end are relative.

This could cause:
- Merge failures (split point not found in relative offsets) → layers appear un-merged → wrong LA rule
- False merges (relative offset happens to match an absolute split point) → distinct layers merged

Investigate:
1. Read how split_info and layer_split_points are constructed (phase1_assembly.py)
2. Determine if offsets in split chunks are absolute or relative
3. Write tests that exercise multi-layer attribution ACROSS a chunk split boundary
4. Verify the merge logic works correctly with split chunks

If the offsets are mismatched, this is a CRITICAL bug. Fix it and add regression tests.
Use real Arabic scholarly text. Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Probe: Pydantic JSON serialization preserves Arabic byte-for-byte
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — Arabic Text Serialization Integrity

Read engines/excerpting/src/writer.py, focusing on write_excerpts() and write_gate_queue().

The CORRUPTION VECTOR: Pydantic's model_dump(mode='json') may apply Unicode normalization (NFC/NFD), escape ZWNJ (U+200C), normalize Arabic presentation forms (U+FB50-U+FDFF), or alter diacritics when serializing to JSON. This would VIOLATE the KR principle: 'Preserve byte-for-byte. No Unicode normalization.'

Write tests:
1. Create an ExcerptRecord with primary_text containing:
   - Arabic text with full tashkeel (fathah, dammah, kasrah, sukun, shadda, tanwin)
   - ZWNJ character (U+200C) between Arabic letters
   - Tatweel (U+0640) inside words
   - Superscript alef (U+0670)
2. Write it through write_excerpts()
3. Read the JSONL back
4. Verify the primary_text is BYTE-FOR-BYTE identical (not just string-equal)
5. Do the same for text_snippet, context_hint, and any other Arabic fields

Also test: footnote text with Arabic, evidence_refs with Arabic snippets, gate_queue Arabic fields.

If serialization alters ANY byte, this is a CRITICAL bug.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Probe: quoted scholars with identical (type, author) pairs
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — Silent Deletion of Duplicate Scholar Layers

Read engines/excerpting/src/phase3_deterministic.py, focusing on compute_quoted_scholars() (F-DET-9).

The CORRUPTION VECTOR: If two SEPARATE text_layers have identical (layer_type, author_canonical_id), the deduplication in compute_quoted_scholars excludes ALL layers matching the primary (type, author). But if a non-primary layer happens to share the same (type, author) as the primary, it gets silently EXCLUDED from quoted_scholars.

Concrete scenario:
- Primary: layer_type=MATN, author_canonical_id='sch_001'
- Layer A: layer_type=SHARH, author_canonical_id='sch_002' (expected: included)
- Layer B: layer_type=MATN, author_canonical_id='sch_001' (same as primary — excluded correctly)
- Layer C: layer_type=MATN, author_canonical_id='sch_003' (different author, same type as primary — should this be included?)

Verify:
1. Does the exclusion work correctly for all combinations?
2. What if two non-primary layers have identical (type, author)? Are they deduplicated correctly?
3. What if the primary has author_canonical_id=None? Does the exclusion use None matching?

Write targeted tests for each scenario.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Probe: evidence markers inside footnotes vs main text
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — Evidence Detection Scope

Read engines/excerpting/src/phase3_deterministic.py, focusing on detect_evidence() (F-DET-5) and the orchestrator at run_phase3_deterministic().

The CORRUPTION VECTOR: Evidence detection runs on primary_text (the teaching unit's text). But a footnote can contain 'رواه البخاري' (narrated by Bukhari) or 'أخرجه مسلم' (reported by Muslim). These footnote markers are in assembled_text but may be outside the primary_text range.

Also check: what happens when a hadith marker like 'رواه' appears as part of a longer word in Arabic? Plain substring matching would match 'رواها' (she narrated) or 'رواهم' (he narrated them) — these are DIFFERENT conjugations.

Write tests:
1. Hadith marker ONLY in a footnote — verify detection behavior
2. Hadith marker in BOTH primary_text and footnote
3. Substring false positive: 'رواها' (different conjugation)
4. Ijma marker 'أجمعوا' inside a quotation

Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Probe: validation bypass paths in deterministic-only mode
tasks.prompt:
KNOWLEDGE CORRUPTION PROBE — Validation Bypass Paths

Read engines/excerpting/src/phase3_validation.py (all validation rules V-P3-1 through V-P3-9).

The CORRUPTION VECTOR: In deterministic-only mode (no LLM client), several fields are left at their default values. The validators must still catch invalid state, but some validators may be conditioned on LLM fields being present.

Investigate:
1. For EACH validator (V-P3-1 through V-P3-9), trace what happens when the excerpt has ONLY deterministic fields (no enrichment, no consensus)
2. Can invalid data pass validation because the validator skips checks when optional fields are None?
3. V-P3-5 (gate consistency) — what if gate_entries reference enrichment fields that don't exist?
4. V-P3-4 (evidence consistency) — what if evidence_refs is empty but there ARE Quran/hadith markers in the text?

Also check: does validation failure halt processing? Per SPEC EX-M-008: gate write failure should HALT.

Write tests for each bypass path found.
Run: python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -x -v --tb=short

tasks.name: Codex independent review of excerpting engine source code
tasks.prompt:
Perform a thorough structural review of the excerpting engine source code in engines/excerpting/src/.

Review ALL files: phase1_assembly.py, phase2_classify.py, phase2_group.py, phase3_deterministic.py, phase3_enrichment.py, phase3_consensus.py, phase3_validation.py, phase3_orchestrator.py, writer.py, pipeline.py, contracts.py.

Check for:
1. Type safety: missing type hints, inconsistent types, unsafe casts
2. Error handling: bare except, swallowed exceptions, missing error paths
3. Pydantic patterns: Field(None) compliance, model_validator usage
4. Test completeness: functions without test coverage
5. Code structure: functions exceeding 50 lines, too many parameters

Do NOT evaluate Arabic text content or domain-specific scholarly decisions.
Write findings to overnight/results/codex-deep-review/review.md

tasks.name: Cross-engine contract and D-023 metadata flow verification
tasks.prompt:
Run the metadata flow verification:
  python3 scripts/verify_metadata_flow.py

Also check cross-engine contracts:
  PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 python tools/check_cross_engine_contracts.py

Report findings to overnight/results/val-contracts/boundaries.md
Do NOT modify any files.

tasks.name: Documentation freshness check
tasks.prompt:
Check documentation freshness:
1. Read NEXT.md and verify it matches the current git HEAD state (run git log --oneline -10)
2. Read engines/excerpting/CLAUDE.md and check if test counts, module list, and state description match actual code
3. Check if engines/excerpting/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md references correct line numbers in source files
4. Verify engines/excerpting/docs/architecture.md describes the actual module structure

Write findings to overnight/results/doc-freshness/report.md
Do NOT modify any files.

tasks.name: Full regression test — ALL engines
tasks.prompt:
Run the full test suite for all completed engines SEPARATELY:
  python -m pytest engines/source/tests/ -v --tb=short
  python -m pytest engines/normalization/tests/ -v --tb=short
  python -m pytest engines/excerpting/tests/ -v --tb=short
Report: total tests, passed, failed, skipped, any error details.
Compare counts against known baseline: source=566, normalization=503 (14 skip), excerpting=593 (2 skip).
If any test count DECREASED, flag as REGRESSION.
Write report to overnight/results/val-test-regression/summary.md
Do NOT modify any files.
